indications: Suspected Opioid Toxicity (Pinpoint Pupils + Low GCS + Hypoventilation)

protocols.name: Hemorrhagic Shock / Massive Bleeding

medications.name: Hexakapron (TXA)
medications.special_instructions: Push slowly over 10 minutes.

medications.name: Hartmann's Solution
medications.special_instructions: Permissive Hypotension: only until radial pulse returns.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid over-resuscitation with fluids.
Check for Tension Pneumothorax if refractory.

protocols.name: Adult Cardiac Arrest

medications.name: Epinephrine 1:10,000
medications.special_instructions: Repeat every 3-5 mins.

medications.name: Amiodarone
medications.special_instructions: Refractory VF/VT only.

contraindications_and_warnings: Minimize CPR interruptions.
Check reversible causes (H's and T's).

protocols.name: General Respiratory Distress

medications.name: Salbutamol (Ventolin)
medications.special_instructions: Nebulized.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid fluid boluses if CHF suspected.
Use caution with high O2 in COPD.

protocols.name: General Cardiac Approach / ACS

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.special_instructions: Chewable.

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.special_instructions: Monitor BP before each dose.

contraindications_and_warnings: Contraindicated if ED meds used recently.
ECG does not rule out MI.

protocols.name: Hemorrhagic Shock Management
immediate_actions: Establish two large-bore IV lines or IO access.
Permissive hypotension approach until major hemorrhage controlled.

medications.name: TXA (Tranexamic Acid)
medications.special_instructions: Administer within 3 hours of injury.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid over-aggressive fluid resuscitation (coagulopathy risk).

protocols.name: Anaphylaxis Management
immediate_actions: Position in supine with legs elevated.

medications.name: Epinephrine 1:1000
medications.special_instructions: Repeat every 5-15 mins if no clinical improvement.

medications.name: Diphenhydramine
medications.special_instructions: H1 receptor antagonist.

contraindications_and_warnings: Epinephrine is first-line; do not delay.

protocols.name: Adult Burn Injury Management
immediate_actions: Cover burns with dry, clean dressings.
Assess for inhalation injury (soot, singed hair, stridor).
Calculate fluid needs based on Rule of Nines.

medications.name: Fentanyl
medications.special_instructions: For analgesic control, titrated to effect.

contraindications_and_warnings: Do not apply ice (hypothermia risk).
Prioritize Airway in facial burns.

protocols.name: General Arrhythmia Management
immediate_actions: If stable: Vagal maneuvers / Pharmacological rate control.

medications.name: Adenosine
medications.special_instructions: Rapid IV push, follow with 20ml flush. May increase to 12mg.

contraindications_and_warnings: Synchronized cardioversion requires sedation if patient conscious.
Caution with Beta-Blockers in bradycardia.

protocols.name: Seizure Management
immediate_actions: Protect patient from injury (remove nearby objects).
Position in left lateral decubitus if possible.

medications.name: Midazolam
medications.special_instructions: For active seizures, may repeat every 5 mins up to 10mg.

contraindications_and_warnings: Status epilepticus: seizures lasting >5 mins or recurrent without recovery.

protocols.name: Diabetic Emergencies

medications.name: Glucose
medications.special_instructions: Administer as 25g of Dextrose 50% solution.

contraindications_and_warnings: Hypoglycemia can mimic stroke or intoxication.
DKA may require insulin and fluids (not in prehospital setting).

protocols.name: Suspected Opioid Toxicity
immediate_actions: Support ventilation with Bag-Valve-Mask (BVM) and 100% oxygen.
Clear airway and place advanced airway if breathing remains inadequate after antidote.

medications.name: Naloxone
medications.special_instructions: Titrate slowly to restore adequate spontaneous respiration, max single dose 2.0mg. Can be given IN if IV access is delayed.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid rushing to full reversal to prevent acute withdrawal syndrome and severe agitation/vomiting.

protocols.name: Acute Hypoglycemia
immediate_actions: Secure airway and support breathing as needed.

medications.name: Glucose
medications.special_instructions: Administer via a secure, free-flowing large-bore IV line. Re-check glucose after 10-15 minutes.

contraindications_and_warnings: Ensure IV patency before administration; extravasation of hypertonic glucose causes severe tissue necrosis.

protocols.name: Acute Pulmonary Edema / Congestive Heart Failure (CHF)
immediate_actions: Sit the patient upright with legs dangling if possible.
Apply Non-Invasive Positive Pressure Ventilation (CPAP) with high-flow oxygen.

medications.name: nitro
medications.special_instructions: Administer every 3-5 minutes as long as systolic BP remains above 100 mmHg.

medications.name: furosemide
medications.special_instructions: Slow IV push. Double the patient's daily home dose if known, up to a max single dose of 120.0mg.

medications.name: isoket
medications.special_instructions: Consider continuous IV infusion if hypertensive crisis persists and SL nitroglycerin is insufficient.

contraindications_and_warnings: Strictly contraindicated if systolic BP < 90 mmHg or if erectile dysfunction medications were taken recently. Avoid fluid boluses.

protocols.name: Cyanide Poisoning & Smoke Inhalation
clinical_triggers: Smoke inhalation in an enclosed space
immediate_actions: Administer 100% high-flow oxygen; consider early intubation for airway burns.

medications.name: cyanokit
medications.special_instructions: Infuse over 15 minutes. A second dose may be required for severe cases.

medications.name: sodium thiosulfate
medications.special_instructions: Administer if Cyanokit is unavailable or as an adjunct in severe cyanide toxicity.

contraindications_and_warnings: Cyanokit causes severe red skin and urine discoloration; this is normal. Do not delay administration in suspected comatose smoke inhalation cases.

protocols.name: Stroke / Suspected Stroke
immediate_actions: Assess airway and breathing; provide high-flow oxygen if hypoxic.
Perform rapid stroke assessment (e.g., FAST exam).
Transport to stroke center with pre-notification.

protocols.name: General Pain Management
immediate_actions: Assess pain level using 1-10 scale or clinical judgment.
Position of comfort and splinting of suspected fractures.
Establish IV/IO access for moderate to severe pain.

medications.name: Fentanyl
medications.special_instructions: Titrate to effect. Preferred in hypovolemia over morphine.

medications.name: Ketamine
medications.special_instructions: For severe, refractory pain. Given as slow push or short infusion.

medications.name: optalgine
medications.special_instructions: For mild to moderate pain.

medications.name: paracetamol
medications.special_instructions: For mild pain or fever.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid Fentanyl in uncorrected severe hypotension.
Avoid Ketamine in suspected severe Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) with high ICP.

protocols.name: Advanced Airway Management / RSI
immediate_actions: Pre-oxygenate with 100% O2 via BVM/Nasal Cannula (Apneic oxygenation).
Prepare suction, advanced airway equipment, and capnography.
Ensure patent and secure IV/IO access.

medications.name: Ketamine
medications.special_instructions: Induction agent of choice for hemodynamically unstable patients or severe asthma.

medications.name: etomidate
medications.special_instructions: Alternative induction agent. Avoid in suspected septic shock.

medications.name: Midazolam
medications.special_instructions: For post-intubation sedation.

contraindications_and_warnings: Ensure all equipment and backup airways are ready before administering induction agents.

protocols.name: Crush Syndrome / Prolonged Entrapment
clinical_triggers: Prolonged entrapment (>1 hour) of muscle mass
immediate_actions: Establish large-bore IV access before extrication if possible.
Aggressive fluid resuscitation to protect kidneys from myoglobinuria.
Apply tourniquet to crushed limb prior to release if severe bleeding or hyperkalemia suspected.
Obtain 12-lead ECG to monitor for hyperkalemia (peaked T waves).

medications.name: Hartmann's Solution
medications.special_instructions: Run wide open before and during extrication.

medications.name: calcium gluconate
medications.special_instructions: Administer if ECG shows signs of hyperkalemia (peaked T waves, wide QRS) immediately upon release.

medications.name: sodium bicarbonate
medications.special_instructions: Alkalinize urine to prevent kidney failure from crush syndrome.

contraindications_and_warnings: Reperfusion injury can cause sudden cardiac arrest upon release. Be prepared with defibrillator and hyperkalemia meds.

protocols.name: Symptomatic Bradycardia
immediate_actions: Assess airway and breathing; administer oxygen.
Prepare for transcutaneous pacing if patient is unstable.

medications.name: Atropine
medications.special_instructions: First-line. Repeat every 3-5 mins up to a maximum of 3mg.

medications.name: dopamine
medications.special_instructions: Use as infusion if atropine is ineffective.

contraindications_and_warnings: Atropine may be ineffective in high-degree AV blocks (Mobitz II or 3rd degree). Proceed directly to pacing or dopamine.

protocols.name: Obstetric Emergencies / Eclampsia
clinical_triggers: Pregnant patient (>20 weeks) or up to 4 weeks postpartum
immediate_actions: Protect airway, prevent injury during seizure.
Place patient in left lateral decubitus position.

medications.name: magnesium sulfate
medications.special_instructions: First-line for eclamptic seizures. Administer slowly over 5-10 minutes.

medications.name: labetalol
medications.special_instructions: For severe hypertension (SBP >160 or DBP >110). Administer slow IV push.

contraindications_and_warnings: Do NOT use Midazolam as first-line for eclamptic seizures unless Magnesium is unavailable or ineffective.

protocols.name: Nausea and Vomiting
clinical_triggers: Severe nausea preventing oral medication intake
immediate_actions: Establish IV access if severe dehydration is suspected.

medications.name: ondansetron
medications.special_instructions: Administer slow IV push. May also be given ODT (orally disintegrating).

contraindications_and_warnings: Use caution in patients with known prolonged QT interval.

protocols.name: Organophosphate / Nerve Agent Exposure
clinical_triggers: SLUDGEM symptoms (Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defecation, GI distress, Emesis, Miosis)
immediate_actions: Ensure full rescuer decontamination before handling patient.
Aggressive airway suctioning for severe secretions.
Support ventilation and apply high-flow oxygen.

medications.name: Atropine
medications.special_instructions: Repeat every 3-5 minutes until bronchorrhea clears and secretions dry up. Can be given IM if no IV access.

contraindications_and_warnings: Atropine dosing in nerve agent toxicity is titrated to the clearing of lung secretions, NOT to the heart rate. Tachycardia is NOT a contraindication here.

protocols.name: Sepsis / Septic Shock
immediate_actions: Assess airway and breathing; administer high-flow oxygen if hypoxic.
Aggressive fluid resuscitation (30ml/kg) for hypotension or shock.

medications.name: dopamine
medications.special_instructions: Use as vasopressor if patient remains hypotensive after fluid resuscitation.

contraindications_and_warnings: Early recognition and aggressive fluid resuscitation are key. Do not delay transport for vasopressors.

protocols.name: Severe Asthma Exacerbation
immediate_actions: Apply Non-Invasive Positive Pressure Ventilation (CPAP) if available.

medications.name: Salbutamol (Ventolin)
medications.special_instructions: Nebulized. Repeat every 20 minutes as needed.

medications.name: solumedrol
medications.special_instructions: Administer as a single dose for severe exacerbations.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid fluid boluses in severe asthma exacerbations due to risk of worsening pulmonary edema.

protocols.name: STEMI Management

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.special_instructions: Chewable.

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.special_instructions: Monitor BP before each dose.

medications.name: heparin
medications.special_instructions: Administer as a single bolus for STEMI patients going for PCI.

contraindications_and_warnings: Contraindicated if ED meds used recently.
ECG does not rule out MI.

protocols.name: Atrial Fibrillation with Rapid Ventricular Response (RVR)

medications.name: Diltiazem
medications.special_instructions: Administer as slow IV bolus over 2 minutes. May repeat at 0.35 mg/kg after 15 minutes if needed.

contraindications_and_warnings: Synchronized cardioversion requires sedation if patient conscious.
Caution with Beta-Blockers in bradycardia.

protocols.name: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) Management
clinical_triggers: Head trauma with altered mental status
immediate_actions: Ensure airway patency and support breathing.
Administer high-flow oxygen to maintain SpO2 > 94%.
Elevate head of bed to 30 degrees if no spinal injury suspected.

medications.name: mannitol
medications.special_instructions: Administer as a single bolus for signs of increased ICP. Use with caution in hypotensive patients.

contraindications_and_warnings: Avoid hyperventilation (PaCO2 < 30 mmHg) as it can reduce cerebral perfusion.
Mannitol can worsen hypotension; ensure adequate volume status before administration.
